title: eulophia aculeata subsp. huttonii
intro: Eulophia aculeata subsp. huttonii is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Eulophia aculeata orchid native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Eulophia aculeata subsp. huttonii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are traditionally used by indigenous people in New Zealand for their medicinal and spiritual value.
